title: Windows: binfmt / qemu-user-static support
body:
### Issue Description

I noticed using podman 5.8.0 it's not possible to run e.g. a aarch64 container on a amd64/x86_64 machine.
Furthermore I noticed that the `systemd-binfmt` service is failed.

A short troubleshooting sesssion showed that the `binfmt_misc` mount is missing:
```
mount -t binfmt_misc binfmt_misc /proc/sys/fs/binfmt_misc
systemctl restart systemd-binfmt
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Install podman on Windows
2. `podman trace machine init --rootful`
3. `podman machine start`
4. `podman machine ssh`
5. `systemctl status systemd-binfmt`
6. `cd /proc/sys/fs/`


### Describe the results you received

```bash
$ cd /proc/sys/fs/binfmt_misc
ls: cannot access 'binfmt_misc/': Too many levels of symbolic links
```

```bash
$ systemctl status systemd-binfmt
× systemd-binfmt.service - Set Up Additional Binary Formats
     Loaded: loaded (/usr/lib/systemd/system/systemd-binfmt.service; static)
    Drop-In: /usr/lib/systemd/system/service.d
             └─10-timeout-abort.conf
     Active: failed (Result: exit-code) since Mon 2026-03-02 09:52:07 CET; 2min 18s ago
 Invocation: d52097e6538d424dabc7ec0d54af11e5
       Docs: man:systemd-binfmt.service(8)
             man:binfmt.d(5)
             https://docs.kernel.org/admin-guide/binfmt-misc.html
             https://systemd.io/API_FILE_SYSTEMS
   Main PID: 74 (code=exited, status=1/FAILURE)
   Mem peak: 1.4M
        CPU: 10ms

Mar 02 09:52:07 ... systemd[1]: Starting systemd-binfmt.service - Set Up Additional Binary Formats...
Mar 02 09:52:07 ... systemd-binfmt[74]: Failed to check if /proc/sys/fs/binfmt_misc is mounted: Too many levels of symbolic links
Mar 02 09:52:07 ... systemd[1]: systemd-binfmt.service: Main process exited, code=exited, status=1/FAILURE
Mar 02 09:52:07 ... systemd[1]: systemd-binfmt.service: Failed with result 'exit-code'.
Mar 02 09:52:07 ... systemd[1]: Failed to start systemd-binfmt.service - Set Up Additional Binary Formats.
```

```bash
$ podman run --rm --tty --interactive --platform linux/arm64/v8 docker.io/library/busybox
{"msg":"exec container process `/bin/sh`: Exec format error","level":"error","time":"2026-03-02T08:55:35.873841Z"}
```

### Describe the results you expected


```
podman machine ssh
# within wsl
mount -t binfmt_misc binfmt_misc /proc/sys/fs/binfmt_misc
systemctl restart systemd-binfmt
```

After that binfmt_misc works as expected

### podman info output

```yaml
podman --version
podman version 5.8.0
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
I have an assumption what's going on.
WSL seems to mount `/proc/sys/fs/binfmt_misc` per default on my machine (most probably because of WSLInterop). Then systemd mounts another automount filesystem on top. This seems to break the binfmt_misc .


```
$ mount
...
binfmt_misc on /proc/sys/fs/binfmt_misc type binfmt_misc (rw,relatime)
...
systemd-1 on /proc/sys/fs/binfmt_misc type autofs (rw,relatime,fd=37,pgrp=1,timeout=0,minproto=5,maxproto=5,direct,pipe_ino=96733)
...
```
